_comment: BASE CASE 2 of 2 — Meridian Sands Technologies Ltd. The DRIFT HERO: a non-stationary ADGM startup that silently re-tiers LOW -> HIGH.
_role_in_demo: Demonstrates the STRENGTH OF THE DRIFT MODEL on the hardest case — a startup that pivots fast on sparse signals. This is where the 'wow' (AI Intelligence 25%) is earned.
_disclaimer: ENTIRELY FICTIONAL composite. Name, registration number, people and events are invented to illustrate a realistic ADGM-startup drift pattern. Any resemblance to a real company is coincidental.
_real_vs_simulated: 100% SIMULATED. Because the entity is fictional, ALL signals are authored (this is the model-strength case, NOT the public-source case — Coinbase carries the real-sourcing story). Mira authors matching EvidenceEvents; nothing here is fetched.
_structure_note: entity_profile = static identity. assertions = the onboarding beliefs that drift breaks over ~30 months.
purpose_of_relationship: Operating account & treasury for an early-stage ADGM-registered fintech (SIMULATED).
_drift_arc: Onboards 2023 as a clean UAE-only B2B SaaS analytics startup (LOW, score 28). Over 2023-2026 it pivots to crypto/Web3 trading infra, opens offshore entities, takes a new >25% foreign investor, breaches its volume envelope, and a co-founder is named in a regional investigation — silently re-tiering to HIGH (~82). No single event alone is decisive; the TRAJECTORY is.

risk_model._note: The rolled-up OUTPUT. Demo headline: 28 -> 82, with no single alarm tripping at onboarding.

kyc_review._note: LOW startup -> long 36-month cadence. THIS IS THE TRAP: the next scheduled review is 2026 — the drift would go unseen for 3 years without continuous monitoring. Detected drift must pull the review off-cycle.
kyc_review._derivation: LOW -> 36mo -> next review 2026-01-15. The whole pitch: a static 3-year clock misses 30 months of silent drift; our system collapses it to 'now'.

entity_profile.legal_form: ADGM Private Company Limited by Shares
entity_profile.registered_address: Al Maryah Island, ADGM, Abu Dhabi, UAE (SIMULATED)
entity_profile.regulated_financial_entity: No — ADGM commercial licence only; NOT FSRA-authorised for financial services at onboarding

assertions.value: B2B SaaS — financial-data analytics dashboards for regional SMEs
assertions.notes: BREAKS — pivots to crypto/Web3 trading infrastructure by 2025. The headline business-model contradiction.
BREACHED — opens offshore entities (e.g. BVI / Seychelles) and operations in higher-risk corridors. Geographic drift.
BREAKS — a new foreign investment fund takes >25% in 2024, diluting founders and introducing a PEP-adjacent UBO. Ownership drift.

assertions.value: SaaS analytics subscriptions only; no trading or custody services
assertions.notes: BREAKS — launches a crypto trading/brokerage product line. Pulls the entity toward a regulated activity.

assertions.value: No corporate digital-asset treasury; crypto out of scope
assertions.notes: BREAKS — adopts a crypto treasury and holds BTC/ETH/stablecoins. AMINA-specific contradiction.
Historic wealth basis; the new investor's funds introduce a fresh SoW question.
BREAKS — funds increasingly originate from crypto trading proceeds, not subscriptions.
BREACHED — monthly flows jump an order of magnitude post-pivot. Behavioural-envelope drift.

assertions.value: ADGM commercial licence only; not FSRA-authorised
assertions.notes: BREAKS — offering crypto brokerage may require FSRA authorisation; operating outside licensed scope is a compliance red flag.

assertions.value: No PEP among founders or UBOs
assertions.notes: BREAKS — 2024 investor has a PEP-adjacent connection, surfacing only after the ownership change.
Stays clean on direct designation, but offshore/corridor expansion raises indirect sanctions-nexus risk.

assertions.value: No adverse media; no investigation or litigation
assertions.notes: BREAKS — a co-founder is named in a regional fraud investigation in 2025/26. The late, decisive adverse-media signal.
THE ROLLED-UP OUTPUT. 28 = LOW. The demo shows this climbing to ~82 (HIGH) across the drift arc — with the 3-year review clock blind to all of it until our system fires.
